name: Temporalis
blurb: From temporal fossa to coronoid process and ramus of mandible; elevates and retracts the mandible (closes jaw).
pearl: Mandibular division of trigeminal (CN V3) via deep temporal nerves. Posterior fibers retract jaw; wasting hollows the temple above the zygomatic arch.

name: Masseter
blurb: From zygomatic arch to lateral ramus and angle of mandible; powerful elevator of the mandible (closes jaw).
pearl: Mandibular division of trigeminal (CN V3) via masseteric nerve. Generates the strongest bite force of the masticatory muscles; easily palpated on clenching.

name: Sternocleidomastoid
blurb: From manubrium and medial clavicle to mastoid process; ipsilateral tilt with contralateral rotation, plus bilateral neck flexion.
pearl: Spinal accessory nerve (CN XI) for motor, with C2-C3 proprioception. Spasm causes torticollis; divides the anterior and posterior cervical triangles.

name: Trapezius
blurb: From external occipital protuberance, nuchal ligament, and C7-T12 spinous processes to clavicle, acromion, and scapular spine; elevates, retracts, depresses, and rotates scapula.
pearl: Motor: spinal accessory nerve (CN XI); C3-C4 proprioception. Posterior triangle injury (e.g., node biopsy) causes shoulder droop and weak overhead abduction.

name: Deltoid
blurb: From lateral clavicle, acromion, and scapular spine to deltoid tuberosity of humerus; chief abductor (15-90 deg); anterior fibers flex/medially rotate, posterior extend/laterally rotate.
pearl: Axillary nerve (C5-C6). Vulnerable to surgical neck fracture or anterior dislocation; lesion causes deltoid atrophy, loss of abduction, and regimental-badge sensory loss.

name: Pectoralis major
blurb: From medial clavicle, sternum, and upper costal cartilages to crest of greater tubercle (lateral lip of bicipital groove); adducts, medially rotates, and flexes the humerus.
pearl: Lateral (C5-C7) and medial (C8-T1) pectoral nerves. Forms anterior axillary fold; clavicular vs sternocostal head crossover gives its twisted, bilaminar insertion.

name: Serratus anterior
blurb: From outer surfaces of ribs 1-8/9 to costal (deep) surface of medial border of scapula; protracts scapula and rotates it upward for overhead abduction.
pearl: Long thoracic nerve (C5-C7). Injury (axillary surgery, trauma) causes winged scapula and inability to raise the arm above horizontal.

name: Latissimus dorsi
blurb: From T7-L5 spinous processes, thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, and lower ribs to floor of bicipital groove; extends, adducts, and medially rotates the humerus.
pearl: Thoracodorsal nerve (C6-C8). The 'handcuff/climbing muscle'; forms posterior axillary fold and crosses the inferior angle of the scapula.

name: Infraspinatus
blurb: From infraspinous fossa of scapula to middle facet of greater tubercle of humerus; laterally (externally) rotates the arm as a rotator cuff muscle.
pearl: Suprascapular nerve (C5-C6). Tested by resisted external rotation; commonly torn in cuff disease, giving a positive external rotation lag sign (lift-off tests subscapularis).

name: Teres major
blurb: From inferior angle/lower lateral border of scapula to crest of lesser tubercle (medial lip of bicipital groove); adducts and medially rotates the humerus.
pearl: Lower subscapular nerve (C5-C6). Forms the inferior border of the quadrangular space and helps bound the triangular space (the 'lat's little helper').

name: Biceps brachii
blurb: Long head from supraglenoid tubercle, short head from coracoid process -> radial tuberosity & bicipital aponeurosis; flexes elbow, supinates forearm.
pearl: Musculocutaneous nerve (C5-C6). 'Popeye' sign from long-head tendon rupture; biceps reflex tests C5-C6 (mainly C5); strongest supinator with elbow flexed.

name: Brachioradialis
blurb: Lateral supracondylar ridge of humerus -> styloid process of radius; flexes elbow, strongest in mid-pronation ('hammer' grip).
pearl: Radial nerve (C5-C6) — the only flexor it supplies. Forms lateral border of cubital fossa; brachioradialis reflex tests C5-C6; inverted reflex suggests C5/C6 cord lesion.

name: Triceps brachii
blurb: Long head from infraglenoid tubercle, lateral & medial heads from humerus -> olecranon of ulna; main extensor of the elbow.
pearl: Radial nerve (C6-C8, mainly C7). Triceps reflex tests C7; radial nerve runs in the spiral (radial) groove — at risk in midshaft humeral fracture.

name: Forearm flexors (anterior compartment)
blurb: Mostly from medial epicondyle (common flexor origin) -> carpals, metacarpals and phalanges; flex wrist and digits, pronate forearm.
pearl: Median nerve (C6-T1), except FCU and medial half of FDP (ulnar nerve, C8-T1). Median nerve runs between FDS and FDP; medial epicondylitis = 'golfer's elbow.'

name: Forearm extensors (posterior compartment)
blurb: Mostly from lateral epicondyle (common extensor origin) -> metacarpals and phalanges; extend wrist and digits, supinate, abduct thumb.
pearl: Radial nerve & its posterior interosseous branch (C5-T1; PIN mainly C7-C8). Lateral epicondylitis = 'tennis elbow'; high radial palsy causes wrist drop, PIN lesion spares wrist extension.

name: Rectus abdominis
blurb: Pubic crest/symphysis -> xiphoid process and costal cartilages 5-7; flexes trunk and compresses abdomen.
pearl: Anterior rami T7-T12 (thoracoabdominal nn.). Tendinous intersections form the 'six-pack'; lies in rectus sheath. Inferior epigastric a. runs deep within sheath.

name: External oblique
blurb: Ribs 5-12 (outer surface) -> linea alba, pubic tubercle, iliac crest; compresses abdomen and rotates trunk to opposite side.
pearl: Anterior rami T7-T12 (incl. subcostal T12). Fibers run 'hands-in-pockets.' Free lower border rolls into inguinal ligament (ASIS to pubic tubercle).

name: Erector spinae
blurb: Common tendon on sacrum/iliac crest/lumbar spinous processes -> ribs, transverse/spinous processes, mastoid; extends and laterally flexes spine.
pearl: Posterior (dorsal) rami of spinal nerves, segmental. Columns I Love Spaghetti = Iliocostalis, Longissimus, Spinalis (lateral to medial). Epaxial postural muscle.

name: Iliopsoas
blurb: Iliacus (iliac fossa) + psoas major (T12-L5 bodies/discs/transverse processes) to lesser trochanter; chief hip flexor.
pearl: Psoas: anterior rami L1-L3; iliacus: femoral nerve L2-L4. Psoas abscess (TB/Crohn) gives a positive psoas sign with hip-extension pain.

name: Tensor fasciae latae
blurb: From ASIS/anterior iliac crest to the iliotibial tract; tenses the IT band and assists hip flexion, abduction, and medial rotation.
pearl: Superior gluteal nerve (L4-S1). With glute medius/minimus it steadies the pelvis in gait; weakness contributes to Trendelenburg.

name: Sartorius
blurb: From ASIS to medial tibia (pes anserinus); flexes, abducts, and laterally rotates the hip and flexes the knee.
pearl: Femoral nerve (L2-L3). Longest muscle in the body; the 'tailor's muscle' producing the cross-legged sitting position.

name: Rectus femoris
blurb: From AIIS (and supra-acetabular ilium) to tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament; extends the knee and flexes the hip.
pearl: Femoral nerve (L2-L4). Only biarticular quad head; weakest as a knee extensor when the hip is already flexed (active insufficiency).

name: Vastus lateralis
blurb: From greater trochanter/lateral lip of linea aspera to tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament; extends the knee.
pearl: Femoral nerve (L2-L4). Largest quad component and a common IM injection site (lateral mid-thigh) in infants and adults.

name: Vastus medialis
blurb: From medial lip of linea aspera to tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament; extends knee, VMO oblique fibers stabilize the patella.
pearl: Femoral nerve (L2-L4). VMO atrophies fast after knee injury; weakness allows lateral patellar maltracking and subluxation.

name: Adductor longus
blurb: From body of pubis to middle third of linea aspera; adducts and assists flexion of the thigh.
pearl: Obturator nerve (L2-L4). Forms the medial border of the femoral triangle; common site of groin strain ('rider's strain').

name: Adductor magnus
blurb: From ischiopubic ramus + ischial tuberosity to linea aspera and adductor tubercle; powerful adductor with a hamstring part.
pearl: Adductor part: obturator nerve (L2-L4); hamstring part: tibial division of sciatic (L4). Adductor hiatus transmits femoral vessels to popliteal.

name: Gracilis
blurb: From body/inferior ramus of pubis to medial tibia (pes anserinus); adducts the thigh and flexes/medially rotates the knee.
pearl: Obturator nerve (L2-L3). Most superficial medial muscle; commonly harvested as a graft for tendon/free-flap reconstruction.

name: Gluteus maximus
blurb: From ilium, sacrum, sacrotuberous ligament to gluteal tuberosity + IT tract; chief hip extensor and lateral rotator.
pearl: Inferior gluteal nerve (L5-S2). Powers rising/stair-climbing; safe IM injection in the upper outer quadrant avoids the sciatic nerve.

name: Gluteus medius
blurb: From outer ilium between gluteal lines to greater trochanter; abducts and medially rotates the hip, steadies pelvis in gait.
pearl: Superior gluteal nerve (L4-S1). Injury causes a positive Trendelenburg sign: pelvis drops on the contralateral (unsupported) side.

name: Iliotibial tract (IT band)
blurb: Thickened longitudinal band of fascia lata from iliac tubercle to Gerdy's tubercle on the lateral tibia; stabilizes the knee laterally.
pearl: Receives gluteus maximus and TFL insertions. IT band syndrome (lateral knee pain) is a common overuse injury in runners and cyclists.

name: Biceps femoris
blurb: Long head from ischial tuberosity, short head from linea aspera, to head of fibula; flexes knee, laterally rotates leg, extends hip.
pearl: Long head: tibial division of sciatic (L5-S2); short head: common fibular division (L5-S2). Forms the lateral border of the popliteal fossa.

name: Semitendinosus
blurb: From ischial tuberosity to medial tibia (pes anserinus); extends the hip and flexes/medially rotates the knee.
pearl: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5-S2). Its long cord-like tendon is a classic ACL reconstruction autograft source.

name: Semimembranosus
blurb: From ischial tuberosity to medial tibial condyle; extends the hip and flexes/medially rotates the knee.
pearl: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5-S2). A Baker's (popliteal) cyst classically arises in the semimembranosus-gastrocnemius bursa.

name: Tibialis anterior
blurb: From lateral tibia/interosseous membrane to medial cuneiform & base of 1st metatarsal; dorsiflexes and inverts the foot.
pearl: Deep fibular (peroneal) nerve, L4-L5. Foot drop with deep fibular injury; strongest dorsiflexor; loss causes steppage gait.

name: Gastrocnemius
blurb: Two heads from femoral condyles to calcaneus via calcaneal tendon; plantarflexes foot and flexes knee.
pearl: Tibial nerve, S1-S2. Crosses knee + ankle; "tennis leg" = medial head tear; ankle-jerk (S1) reflex muscle.

name: Soleus
blurb: From soleal line of tibia/posterior fibula to calcaneus via calcaneal tendon; powerful plantarflexor of the foot.
pearl: Tibial nerve, S1-S2. Calf "peripheral heart" muscle pump; key DVT site (soleal venous plexus); maintains standing posture.

name: Fibularis longus (peroneus longus)
blurb: From head/upper lateral fibula, crosses sole to medial cuneiform & 1st metatarsal base; everts and plantarflexes foot.
pearl: Superficial fibular (peroneal) nerve, L5-S1. Supports transverse & lateral longitudinal arches; nerve vulnerable at fibular neck.

name: Calcaneal (Achilles) tendon
blurb: Shared tendon of gastrocnemius and soleus (triceps surae) inserting on the calcaneal tuberosity; powerful ankle plantarflexion.
pearl: Driven by tibial nerve (S1-S2) muscles. Strongest/thickest tendon in body; rupture gives positive Thompson test (no plantarflexion on calf squeeze).
